description: Security decisions must be enforced in trusted server/runtime code.
body: UI checks and client validation are usability aids, not security boundaries. Use when secure coding work touches trusted boundaries, dangerous sinks, authorization checks, secret flows, and negative-path tests. Do not use this guidance to override a more specific pack, user instruction, or repository contract. Actions: choose the smallest direction that preserves the domain contract; inspect the current files first, keep the change scoped to the owning boundary, and record what evidence changed the decision. Validation signals include server-side deny tests, sink-specific payload tests, secret redaction checks, dependency evidence, and audit notes. Risk boundary: escalate or stop when the task involves client-only enforcement, secret disclosure, injection, permissive fallback, and unsafe shortcuts, missing owner evidence, or live external state.
